message.text: Hey! You're really good at this.
message.hint: A kind word on its own is fine. Look at what they're asking for…

message.text: What school do you go to?
message.found: Nice eye! Asking about your school is a red flag. 🚩

spot.prompt: Someone we don't know just sent this. Something in it isn't safe to answer — can you tap it?
spot.missHint: Hmm, look again — which part feels a bit too personal, or pushy?

decide.prompt: Now — what should we do about it?

choices.feedback: Careful! Your school is a big clue about where to find you in real life. That's not something to share with someone you only know online.

choices.text: Don't answer — tell a grown-up I trust
choices.feedback: Great choice! Telling a trusted adult is always safe — and you are never in trouble for asking for help.

choices.text: Ask what school THEY go to
choices.feedback: It feels fair to swap, but you still can't be sure who is really typing. It's safer not to trade personal details at all.

react.takeaway: Where you go to school is private information, even if the person seems friendly.
react.parentTag: asking where you go to school
